description: Employment information for a single case note.

anyOf.description: Use when the document is NOT relevant.

is_relevant.description: Whether this document contains information relevant to: Extract employment-related information from supervision case notes, including current employment status, job search activity, and details about current employers. Playground collection run against fictional US_OZ data to exercise the extraction platform; never run against real state data.

anyOf.description: Use when the document IS relevant.

is_relevant.description: Whether this document contains information relevant to: Extract employment-related information from supervision case notes, including current employment status, job search activity, and details about current employers. Playground collection run against fictional US_OZ data to exercise the extraction platform; never run against real state data.

adversarial_interpretation.description: The strongest alternative reading of the source text — how one could reasonably arrive at a different value, or why a value might be present when none was extracted. Null when no reasonable alternative exists.

value.description:
The person's primary employment status as described in the case note. Allowed values:
  - employed: The person currently has a job, including self-employment, gig, day labor, or cash work.
  - unemployed: The person is explicitly not currently working.
  - other: A status that is neither clearly employed nor unemployed (e.g. receiving non-employment income).

confidence_level.description:
The evidence quality behind this field's value. Allowed values:
  - speculative: Lowest confidence: a plausible alternative reading of the document exists (i.e. the model recorded an adversarial interpretation), so there is genuine doubt about whether the extracted value is correct.
  - inferred: The value follows from one clear inferential step over direct evidence in the document; there is no adversarial interpretation.
  - explicit: The value is directly stated in the document, with at most minor normalization (synonym, abbreviation, or paraphrase).
  - verbatim: Highest confidence: the value appears exactly as-is in the document — the citation text IS the value, with zero rewording or substitution.

text.description: The exact quoted text from the document.

start.description: Character offset in the document where the quoted text begins.

end.description: Character offset in the document where the quoted text ends.

adversarial_interpretation.description: The strongest alternative reading of the source text — how one could reasonably arrive at a different value, or why a value might be present when none was extracted. Null when no reasonable alternative exists.

null_reason.description:
Why no value could be extracted for this field. Allowed values:
  - not_applicable: The field does not apply given the values of other fields (e.g. `employer_name` when `primary_status` is "unemployed").
  - no_info_found: The document does not mention this information.
  - explicitly_unknown: The document acknowledges the information but states it is unknown.

value.description:
Whether the person is actively searching for employment. Allowed values:
  - searching: The person is actively looking for work (applications, job fairs, workforce referrals).
  - not_searching: The person is explicitly not looking for work.
  - other: Search activity is mentioned but does not fit searching or not_searching.

employment_note_summary.description: One-sentence summary of the employment-related content of the note, composed by the model rather than extracted from the text.

employers.description: List of current employers mentioned in the case note. A person who runs their own venture is employed by that venture with employment_type=self_employed.

value.description:
Type of employment. Allowed values:
  - employee_ft: A full-time employee (stated explicitly as full-time).
  - employee_pt: A part-time employee (stated explicitly as part-time).
  - self_employed: The person runs their own business, freelances, or is otherwise self-employed.
  - gig: App-based or platform work (e.g. DoorDash, Uber, TaskRabbit).
  - temp_agency: Employment through a staffing or temp agency.
  - other: An employment type that does not fit any of the above.

value.description:
Time period for the pay rate. Allowed values:
  - hourly: The pay rate is per hour.
  - daily: The pay rate is per day.
  - weekly: The pay rate is per week.
  - monthly: The pay rate is per month.
  - annually: The pay rate is per year.

employment_changes.description: Employment changes mentioned in the case note (e.g. started or lost a job).

items.description: A single element of the array.

adversarial_interpretation.description: The strongest alternative reading of the source text — how one could reasonably arrive at a different value, or why a value might be present when none was extracted. Null when no reasonable alternative exists.

value.description:
Type of employment change. Allowed values:
  - started_job: The person started a new job.
  - lost_job: The person was fired, laid off, or otherwise lost a job.
  - suspended: The person was suspended from a job.
  - other: An employment change that does not fit any of the above.
